notes: Germany should score German as the official language while giving strong English credit because the national tourism board explicitly says a large proportion of the population speaks English and that language barriers should not pose a problem for many visitors.

evidence.title: Germany Travel: Information on Germany
evidence.note: Germany's national tourism board says the official language is German and that a large proportion of the population also speaks English, so language barriers should not pose a problem for many international visitors.

evidence.title: Britannica: Germany
evidence.note: Britannica identifies German as the official language of Germany.

notes: The Netherlands should score Dutch as the national language while giving very high English credit because official visitor guidance says English is widely spoken throughout the country and around 90% of the population speaks it.

evidence.title: Government of the Netherlands: Civic integration
evidence.note: Government guidance treats Dutch as the standard language newcomers are expected to learn, supporting Dutch as the national default language.

evidence.title: I amsterdam: Visitor information
evidence.note: Official visitor guidance says English is widely spoken in Amsterdam and throughout the Netherlands, with approximately 90% of the population speaking it.

notes: Norway should score Norwegian as the national language while giving strong English credit because official travel guidance says most Norwegians speak English.

evidence.title: Visit Norway: Travel tips A-Z
evidence.note: Visit Norway says most Norwegians speak English, especially younger people.

evidence.title: Britannica: Norway
evidence.note: Britannica identifies Norwegian as the principal language of Norway.
